item.title: Why squeezing 70GW into German waters is 'nonsense' – Skyborn exec
item.description: Global head of JV & asset management, Jan Engelbert, also speaks out against rigid regulation for overplanting wind projects at sea

item.title: IEA: Tougher investment case for wind as costs and curtailment squeeze returns – storage becomes key
item.description: Despite financial challenges, investments in wind are expected to reach record levels this year, according to a new report from the International Energy Agency (IEA)

item.title: Vestas hires senior manager from rival Siemens Gamesa
item.description: Siemens Gamesa veteran is joining Vestas as senior vice president of nacelle manufacturing after 16 years at German wind turbine manufacturer

item.title: Eni deal looks to put Italy on the map for battery production
item.description: Eni has signed a partnership with Italian battery firm Seri Industrial to develop the full battery supply chain

item.title: Seven takeaways from Dajin prospectus as Chinese giant targets Europe
item.description: Dajin's new strategy, dubbed Blue Ocean, aims to outflank competitors by offering end-to-end solutions while carving out a lead in developing deep-water markets.

item.title: US offshore wind faces fresh Trump threat from inspection fee plan
item.description: Proposal by Interior Department would add millions in costs for operating projects, far higher than equivalent oil and gas facilities

item.title: Orsted bolsters top team with Equinor hire
item.description: Equinor veteran joins Orsted as head of operational excellence after 15 years at Norwegian developer

item.title: Deals of the Week: Vestas, Suzlon, EDF, Burger King
item.description: Vestas, Nordex and Suzlon all wrap up wind turbine orders while there are power purchase agreements involving EDF and Burger King, and a big contract for DEME in Japan

item.title: Mingyang partnering on Canadian offshore wind project
item.description: Memorandum of understanding will see Mingyang investigate investing in up to 2GW planned offshore wind farm

item.title: Why Vattenfall boss is sceptical of hybrid offshore wind links – and what he prefers
item.description: Linking offshore wind farms in waters of one country to power grid of another is low-hanging fruit within cross-border cooperation, David Flood tells panel at Berlin conference

item.title: Fired BP chair hits back against 'lies' after bullying accusations
item.description: Manifold also said it is ‘nonsense’ he overstepped his role and appeared to take swipe at previous BP chairs in emphasising his supposed everyman credentials

item.title: Recharge launches 40 Under 40 – nominations now open
item.description: Recharge opens nominations to spotlight young professionals shaping the wind industry's next decade

item.title: Tesla batteries to power up in landmark Australian energy storage project
item.description: RWE's Limondale battery energy storage system uses Tesla Megapacks and is now the longest duration battery operating in Australia

item.title: Corporate clean energy deals could slow down under promised emissions rules change
item.description: Major corporate energy buyers push back against proposed hourly and geographic matching rules in the GHG Protocol

item.title: Traditional carmakers race into energy storage with South Korean suppliers
item.description: Carmakers and their South Korean battery partners are pivoting from slowing EV markets to booming energy storage demand, repurposing factories to supply power-hungry data centres and grids

item.title: Mingyang accepted into another European offshore wind group
item.description: Mingyang welcomed into Norwegian group soon after German acceptance, despite concerns over whether Chinese wind turbines pose a security threat to Europe

item.title: Is TotalEnergies walking away from offshore wind? France suggests otherwise
item.description: Oil & gas major seeks permit for 1.5GW wind farm off Normandy after giving up US sites and doubting conditions for multi-gigawatt German pipeline for wind at sea

item.title: China's Dajin says Europe needs its monopiles to hit offshore wind goals
item.description: Chinese industrial conglomerate eyes global offshore wind market but shuns foundation tenders in China itself

item.title: 'High risk': How the EU raised the stakes over Chinese cleantech
item.description: Putting country of ownership at the heart of policy could have big implications for how wind, solar and energy storage projects are financed and where factories are built

item.title: Vestas and GE Vernova big winners in fire sale of fallen blade giant's assets
item.description: Once valued at nearly $2bn, TPI files wind-down plan that would see GE Vernova and Vestas swoop for key assets at fire sale prices

item.title: Japanese conglomerate strikes UK battery storage deal with Gresham House
item.description: Sumitomo makes UK clean energy push through five-project battery storage deal

item.title: 'Trump won't last forever': US groups press RWE to hold its ground on offshore wind
item.description: Scores of NGOs and other groups tell RWE boss Krebber to 'resist fossil-fueled bullying' and keep faith in offshore wind

item.title: Abrupt BP chair exit leaves investors seeking answers... again
item.description: As another top executive abruptly leaves BP, fresh uncertainty engulfs supermajor's new phase

item.title: Wind CEO criticises country's 'unheard of' failure to honour contracts
item.description: Developers up in arms after Vietnam broke offtake contracts with wind and solar farms

item.title: BP chair removed over 'unacceptable' conduct
item.description: Manifold kicked out over ‘serious concerns’ around governance issues less than a year after being named chair

item.title: TSO calls for offshore wind security spending to count toward Nato defence budget
item.description: Offshore wind resilience measures should count toward mandated NATO spending, German TSO 50Hertz argues

item.title: Nordex boss says Chinese turbine makers should be banned from Europe
item.description: Chinese wind turbine makers should be designated as high-risk under EU cyber rules, argues chief of German supplier Nordex

item.title: Police warn of deadly debris risk as wind turbine burns near German Autobahn
item.description: Police warn of serious injury or loss of life as Enercon machine for unknown reasons burns at wind farm in northern Bavaria

item.title: Cadeler CEO warns over 'weakest link' in offshore wind supply chain
item.description: Vessels giant boss says more resilience needed to prevent ripple effects on projects as sector gears up for 'strong comeback' in Europe

item.title: 'In praise of litigation': US offshore wind lawyer urges strong sector defence
item.description: Developers should use the courts more aggressively to defend offshore wind from hostile federal policy swings, a leading industry lawyer has argued.
